assertions.title: Chat completions logprobs shape is stable

steps.expected: choices[0].logprobs.content is an array of objects with token, logprob, top_logprobs
steps.observed: choices[0].logprobs.content is an array of 6 objects, each with token (string), logprob (number), and top_logprobs (array of {token, logprob}). No legacy fields (tokens, token_logprobs, text_offset) present.

steps.action: POST /v1/chat/completions with logprobs:true, top_logprobs:2, stream:true, max_tokens:5 (streaming)
steps.expected: SSE chunks contain logprobs in content array format
steps.observed: 12 SSE chunks total. 5 logprobs chunks and 5 content chunks interleaved. Each logprobs chunk has delta.logprobs.content as array of objects with token, logprob, top_logprobs.

steps.expected: No tokens, token_logprobs, text_offset fields at logprobs top level
steps.observed: logprobs object only contains 'content' key. No legacy fields present.

evidence.network: POST /v1/chat/completions (non-streaming) -> 200 OK, 1676 bytes; POST /v1/chat/completions (streaming) -> 200 OK, 3098 bytes SSE

assertions.title: Chat completions logprobs do not break tool-call streaming

steps.action: POST /v1/chat/completions with logprobs:true, tools:[get_weather], stream:true (first attempt: casual prompt)
steps.expected: Tool calls emitted with logprobs interleaved
steps.observed: Model responded with text content instead of tool calls (102 chunks, no tool_calls). Logprobs were present throughout. No crash or error.

steps.action: POST /v1/chat/completions with logprobs:true, tools:[get_weather], stream:true, tool_choice:auto (second attempt: explicit system prompt + user instruction)
steps.expected: Tool calls emitted with logprobs interleaved, JSON valid
steps.observed: 23 SSE chunks. Model emitted 2 tool_call deltas (chunks 19, 21) with logprobs chunks interleaved (chunks 18, 20 near tool calls). finish_reason='tool_calls'. Tool call JSON args={"location": " 'Paris'"} is valid and parseable.

steps.action: Validate logprobs and tool_calls interleaving integrity
steps.expected: Logprob chunks do not corrupt tool-call JSON
steps.observed: 3 logprobs chunks in the tool call region (chunks 18, 20). 2 tool_call deltas (chunks 19, 21). Tool call JSON parses correctly. No corruption detected.

evidence.network: POST /v1/chat/completions (attempt 1) -> 200 OK, 25792 bytes SSE; POST /v1/chat/completions (attempt 2) -> 200 OK, 7800 bytes SSE

frictions.description: Model (Gemma-4-26B-A4B-it-JANG_4M) does not reliably emit tool calls with casual prompts. Required explicit system prompt instructing tool use + explicit user instruction to trigger tool calls.
frictions.resolution: Added system message instructing tool use and used 'Use the get_weather function to check the weather in Paris' as the user prompt with tool_choice:auto

frictions.description: curl -s output appears truncated when piping (only 201 bytes captured for 1676-byte response). Response JSON contains control characters (newline in token strings like '<|channel>thought\n') that may interfere with pipe-based processing.
frictions.resolution: Switched to Python urllib.request for reliable full response capture and JSON parsing

summary: Tested 2 assertions: both passed. VAL-CHAT-001: chat completions logprobs use stable content-array format (not legacy flat-array) in both streaming and non-streaming modes. VAL-CHAT-002: logprobs and tool_calls are correctly interleaved in SSE streams without corrupting tool-call JSON parsing.
